name: Fathom
description: Free-leaning AI meeting notetaker with summaries

developer.comment: San Francisco-based company founded by Richard White (Y Combinator W21); raised a $17M Series A in 2024.

first-release.comment: Company incorporated in 2020; the first Zoom-native product was in early access before this. Used the public launch (out of beta) announced Jan 24, 2022 as the first-release date. Checked 2026-06-12.

status.comment: Actively developed and sold; newer bot-free desktop capture rolling out as of June 2026.

license.comment: Closed-source SaaS with a genuinely usable free plan plus paid tiers.

primary-type.comment: Records, transcribes and summarizes live Zoom/Meet/Teams meetings and produces AI notes and action items.

real-time-dictation.comment: Meeting transcriber/notetaker, not a speak-to-type dictation tool.

file-transcription.comment: Vendor states it 'can only record and transcribe live meetings on Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams - we do not support uploaded calls.' File upload is on the roadmap but not available as of June 2026.

live-meeting-capture.comment: Captures live Zoom, Google Meet and Microsoft Teams calls; offers both bot-based recording and a newer bot-free capture option.

streaming-low-latency.comment: New experience provides a live summary and real-time transcription that updates as the meeting goes on; currently limited to Mac with English-language meetings. The older bot-based experience generated transcripts only after the call.

audio-sources.comment: Captures live meeting audio via a meeting bot or newer bot-free (native system-audio) capture; no file upload or URL import.

underlying-engine.comment: Fathom does not publicly disclose its speech-recognition (ASR) engine. It uses Anthropic, OpenAI and Google as AI sub-processors for summarization, but the transcription engine itself is not named.

language-count.comment: Vendor states transcripts are supported in 38 languages.

english-accuracy.comment: No independent WER benchmark found. Reviews cite ~95% transcription accuracy with good audio, but that is a vendor/review claim, not an independent benchmark; left null per guidelines rather than guessing.

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: Detects call language automatically; for Spanish, Portuguese, German, French, Italian or Dutch it auto-translates and displays the summary in that language.

custom-vocabulary.comment: No documented user-facing custom-dictionary/vocabulary-boost feature found in official materials; could not confirm either way.

speaker-diarization.comment: Uses speaker recognition to distinguish and label different speakers in the transcript (occasional attribution mistakes noted in reviews).

timestamps.comment: Provides clickable timestamps linked to recordings so users can jump to specific moments.

ai-summaries-notes.comment: Auto-generates AI summaries, action items and follow-up notes; free plan limited to AI summaries on the first 5 calls/month.

built-in-editor.comment: Supports creating/sharing clips and highlights from recordings, but no documented in-app transcript text editor; could not confirm a true correction editor. Not a media-by-text editor à la Descript.

translation.comment: Can translate summaries to/from Spanish, Portuguese, German, French, Italian and Dutch (summary regenerates in the chosen language); applies to summaries rather than full transcript translation.

export-formats.comment: Native first-party transcript file formats (txt/docx/srt etc.) are not documented in official materials. Bulk export to TXT/CSV/JSON/Markdown is offered via the third-party TranscriptExporter partner, not as a confirmed native feature; left null rather than guessing the native format set.

processing-location.comment: Cloud SaaS; all Fathom data is stored in the United States. SOC2 Type II, HIPAA and GDPR compliant.

offline-capable.comment: Cloud-based service; requires a network connection.

data-used-for-training.comment: Fathom uses de-identified customer data to improve its own AI accuracy by default, but users can opt out via settings (org-wide opt-out for Team Edition). Third-party AI sub-processors (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) are contractually barred from training on user data.

data-deletion-control.comment: Users can delete recordings and accounts; on account deletion all recording data and metadata are removed (backups purged after 7 days).

platforms.comment: Web app plus iOS and Android apps; a native macOS and Windows desktop app powers the newer bot-free capture experience.

system-wide-dictation.comment: Not a dictation tool; no OS-wide speak-to-type.

integrations.comment: Records Zoom, Google Meet and Teams; integrates with Slack, Notion, Asana, Zapier and CRMs (Salesforce, HubSpot). Calendar sync exists but a dedicated Google Calendar integration tag was not separately confirmed, so not included.

api-automation.comment: Offers an API (developers.fathom.ai) plus Zapier automation.

pricing-model.comment: Free tier plus per-user subscription plans (Premium, Team Edition, Team Edition Pro).

free-tier.comment: Genuinely usable free-forever plan, not just a trial.

free-allowance.value: Unlimited recordings, transcripts and storage (Zoom/Meet/Teams); AI summaries, action items and follow-ups limited to the first 5 calls per month; basic Slack/Zapier/CRM integrations

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest paid plan is Premium at ~$15/user/mo billed annually ($19/mo on monthly billing, up from $15 as of March 2026). Checked 2026-06-12.
